task: push the door to the left
raw:
**SCENE ANALYSIS -** A white door is attached to a wooden cabinet. The door has a metallic handle. The cabinet has multiple shelves, some with items and some empty. The door is in a closed position. **STRATEGY -** To push the door to the left, first reach for the door handle, grasp it, then apply a force to the door in the leftward direction. The door must be pushed until it is fully open. **AFFORDANCE REASONING -** Step 1 reach the door body to approach the handle; Step 2 grasp the metallic handle for secure hold; Step 3 push the door in the left direction until it is fully open.

Goal: open(door)
Scene: door, cabinet, handle
Step 1: reach(door)
  affordance_region: the body of the white door
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, door, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, door) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(handle)
  affordance_region: the metallic handle of the white door
  contact: holding(handle) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(handle)
Step 3: push(door -> left)
  affordance_region: the body of the white door
  contact: holding(handle) [progress]
  spatial: distance(door, cabinet, >, 0.1) [completion]
  direction: push_axis(-1, 0, 0) [progress]
  safety: no_slip(handle)
  done_when: distance(door, cabinet) > 0.1
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: the body of the white door
the metallic handle of the white door
the body of the white door
